source_spans.text: Soil temperature has dramatic effects on transpiration and photosynthesis
Soil temperature has dramatic effects on transpiration and photosynthesis

Site preparation, particularly at the higher levels of duff reduction and mineral soil exposure, will contribute to both soil warming and frost protection during much of the growing season.

nodes.name: Decreasing ground cover of subalpine shrubs and herbs

source_spans.text: Decreasing ground cover of subalpine shrubs and herbs has been shown to improve soil temperature regimes
Decreasing ground cover of subalpine shrubs and herbs has been shown to improve soil temperature regimes
In some areas, particularly in steep mountainous terrain. snow creep may be one of the largest contributors to plantation mortality.

nodes.name: Planting near large thermal masses such as stumps and logs

source_spans.text: Planting near large thermal masses such as stumps and logs will help to protect seedlings from frost and winter desiccation.
